skill.name: data-engineer
skill.description: Build scalable data pipelines, modern data warehouses, and
skill.summary: Build scalable data pipelines, modern data warehouses, and
skill.author: unknown

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only documentation and educational content. No executable code, network access, or file system operations detected.

content.user_title: Build scalable data pipelines and modern data architectures
content.value_statement: Provides expert guidance on designing robust, scalable data pipelines using the modern data stack. Covers batch and streaming processing, data warehousing, and cloud-native platforms for efficient analytics infrastructure.
actual_capabilities: Design data lakehouse architectures (Delta Lake, Iceberg, Hudi)
Implement batch processing with Apache Spark and dbt
Build real-time streaming pipelines with Kafka and Flink
Orchestrate workflows using Airflow, Prefect, or Dagster
Model data warehouses using dimensional and Data Vault techniques
Deploy cloud-native data solutions on AWS, Azure, and GCP
Implement data quality, lineage, and governance frameworks
limitations: Not suitable for simple exploratory data analysis without engineering needs
Does not focus on ML model development itself, only the surrounding pipelines
Requires access to data sources and storage systems to function effectively

use_cases.title: Design Modern Data Architecture
use_cases.description: Create scalable lakehouse architectures using Delta Lake or Iceberg on cloud storage to unify data warehousing and analytics.

use_cases.title: Orchestrate Complex ETL Pipelines
use_cases.description: Build and manage reliable data workflows using Airflow or dbt for batch processing, ensuring data quality and lineage.

use_cases.title: Implement Real-Time Streaming
use_cases.description: Set up event-driven architectures with Kafka and Flink for low-latency analytics and real-time feature engineering.

prompt_templates.title: Design ETL Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Moving data from Postgres to Snowflake
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a batch ETL pipeline using Airflow and dbt to ingest data from PostgreSQL into Snowflake, ensuring data quality checks are applied.

prompt_templates.title: Streaming Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Architect a real-time data pipeline using Apache Kafka and Flink to process clickstream events and sink results to ClickHouse.

output_examples.input: Create a dbt model for customer orders

best_practices: Define clear data contracts and SLAs before implementation
Implement data validation and quality checks at every stage
Use idempotent transformations to ensure reproducibility
Enforce least-privilege access controls and protect PII
Monitor pipeline costs and operational reliability continuously
anti_patterns: Hardcoding credentials or sensitive data in pipeline code
Ignoring data skew in distributed processing jobs
Creating monolithic DAGs that are hard to debug and maintain
Skipping data quality checks for the sake of speed

faq.question: When should I use dbt versus Apache Spark?
faq.answer: Use dbt for SQL-based transformations within a data warehouse. Use Spark for heavy data processing, ETL across disparate systems, or when complex logic in Python/Scala is required.

faq.question: How do I handle real-time data latency?
faq.answer: Utilize streaming technologies like Kafka and Flink, and design your architecture for event-driven processing with minimal state management.
